examples: Heihe, in China's Heilongjiang province, bordering Russia, was part of a Japanese puppet state known as Manchukuo in the area of Manchuria at that time.
On old maps, the land is called Outer Manchuria or, even more evocatively, Eastern Tartary.
Many moved south to Shanghai after the Japanese took Manchuria in the early 1930s.
In 1959, a vast new oil field in the grasslands of northeast China, in Manchuria, was discovered.
